message:
feat(pthread): port __pthread_cond_*/__pthread_kill/__pthread_key_delete glibc reserved aliases (bd-huug1)

Adds the remaining glibc reserved-namespace aliases for the
pthread condvar / signal / TSD entry points:

- `__pthread_cond_wait`, `__pthread_cond_signal`,
  `__pthread_cond_broadcast` (delegate to public counterparts)
- `__pthread_kill` (delegates to `pthread_kill`)
- `__pthread_key_delete` (delegates to `pthread_key_delete`)

glibc emits these underscored variants both as internal-use
entry points and as the symbols libstdc++ links against for
std::condition_variable, std::call_once thread cancellation
cleanup, and TSD destructor paths.

Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers
delegating to the public names — same pattern as the
previously-shipped __pthread_create/join/detach/exit aliases.

Tests: 5 abi integration tests covering: (a) __pthread_cond_signal
wakes a waiting thread that uses __pthread_cond_wait; (b)
__pthread_cond_broadcast wakes all 3 waiters; (c)
__pthread_kill(self, 0) returns 0 (existence probe); (d)
__pthread_kill with out-of-range signum returns EINVAL; (e)
__pthread_key_delete on a freshly-created key returns 0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-0u5fm","title":"printf '*' negative width/precision: neg width should left-justify, neg precision should be ignored","description":"In crates/frankenlibc-core/src/stdio/printf.rs, when width comes from '*' argument (Width::FromArg), a negative integer argument is not translated into '-' flag + abs(width) as C11 7.21.6.1 para 5 requires. Surfaced by bd-12hh printf_conformance matrix (sprintf_star_neg_width case: format='%*d', args=[-5, 42] — expected '42   ', got 2000+ spaces). resolve_width() also returns 0 for FromArg which means it never reads the * argument at all; the two bugs compound.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:21:55.984065866Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T06:01:01.293518369Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T06:01:01.291039407Z","close_reason":"extract_va_args! now reads '*' width/precision as c_int (with sign-extension to u64), so render_printf's existing negative-value handling at lines 3139-3163 is reached. sprintf_star_neg_width and sprintf_star_neg_precision now pass end-to-end. Committed 717fae6.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-0ubkr","title":"fuzz: cargo +nightly fuzz run fails to link with libc.map version script","description":"All fuzz_targets in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz fail at link time when invoked via 'cargo +nightly fuzz run'. The frankenlibc-abi cdylib is built with version_scripts/libc.map listing many GLIBC_2.2.5 symbols (memcpy, __errno_location, __libc_start_main, etc) that aren't defined in the fuzz build because the abi is being linked into a libfuzzer dylib rather than the final libc.so. cargo build (no fuzz flags) succeeds — only the cargo-fuzz wrapper that injects -Cpasses=sancov-module + -Zsanitizer=address triggers the linker error. Likely fix: gate the version_scripts via a cfg(not(fuzzing)) in build.rs or expose a fuzz-friendly cargo feature that omits the script. Reproduce: 'cargo +nightly fuzz run --fuzz-dir crates/frankenlibc-fuzz fuzz_dirent -- -max_total_time=5'.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T06:55:53.744354544Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T07:10:12.938149969Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T07:10:12.935039718Z","close_reason":"build.rs now skips applying libc.map version script when CARGO_ENCODED_RUSTFLAGS contains '--cfg=fuzzing'. cargo +nightly fuzz run links cleanly and finds real bugs (proven by bd-m40be). Committed 3b74c87.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-0udd","title":"Metamorphic proptests for SigSet bitset algebra","description":"frankenlibc_core::signal::SigSet is a 64-bit signal bitmask with add/del/is_member/empty/full operations. Unit tests cover point cases but no algebraic MRs fence the bitset semantics. Add: (1) add-then-is_member, (2) del-reverses-add, (3) idempotent add, (4) invalid-sig rejection preserves state, (5) commutative add ordering, (6) full().is_member iff valid_signal. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-core/src/signal/mod.rs #[cfg(test)] module.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T20:24:38.440961830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T20:26:14.520499037Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T20:26:14.518577480Z","close_reason":"Added 7 SigSet MR proptests in commit 38830fa covering algebraic properties: invertive add/del, idempotent add, commutative order, invalid-sig state preservation, full()⇔valid_signal consistency, bulk add+del roundtrip, absorbing del-absent. ~1790 case evaluations per run.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-0ukmy","title":"PORT: __pthread_create / __pthread_join / __pthread_detach / __pthread_exit (glibc reserved aliases)","description":"Port the four glibc reserved-namespace aliases for the pthread thread-lifecycle entry points: __pthread_create, __pthread_join, __pthread_detach, __pthread_exit. glibc emits these underscored variants both as internal-use entry points and as the symbols libstdc++ links against for std::thread / std::async machinery. Without them callers see undefined-reference errors when linking libstdc++-using code statically. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers that delegate to the public pthread_create/join/detach/exit in pthread_abi. Tests verify each alias produces the same observable thread lifecycle as its public counterpart on a worker that returns a sentinel value.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:26:38.619271488Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:26:38.619271488Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Without them callers see undefined-reference errors when linking libstdc++-using code statically. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers that delegate to the public pthread_create/join/detach/exit in pthread_abi. Tests verify each alias produces the same observable thread lifecycle as its public counterpart on a worker that returns a sentinel value.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:26:38.619271488Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:30:43.015367309Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T11:30:43.013385544Z","close_reason":"shipped: __pthread_create/join/detach/exit glibc reserved-namespace aliases delegating to existing public-name impls + 4 abi tests covering full create/exit/join round-trip with retval propagation, all green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-0ul0z","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] getrlimit/setrlimit reject 9 standard Linux RLIMIT_* values","description":"FOUND + FIXED via differential harness vs glibc (commit 6c5c98ac).

frankenlibc_core::resource::valid_resource enumerated only a sparse subset of rlimit identifiers (CPU/FSIZE/DATA/STACK/CORE/NOFILE/AS), causing getrlimit and setrlimit to return EINVAL for RLIMIT_RSS, RLIMIT_NPROC, RLIMIT_MEMLOCK, RLIMIT_LOCKS, RLIMIT_SIGPENDING, RLIMIT_MSGQUEUE, RLIMIT_NICE, RLIMIT_RTPRIO, and RLIMIT_RTTIME — all 9 valid Linux rlimits.

Reachability: any program that queries per-user process limits, queued-signal budget, mlock allowance, file-locks limit, message-queue size, nice ceiling, or RT scheduling limits would get EINVAL where they expected a real kernel value. ulimit shell builtin, container runtimes, JVM init, etc.

Fix: replace sparse `matches!` allowlist with contiguous range check (RLIMIT_CPU..=RLIMIT_RTTIME). Add the 9 missing constants. Update unit test to cover all 11 standard rlimits.

Severity: HIGH — silent EINVAL on valid kernel resources.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T07:00:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T07:00:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T07:00:00Z","close_reason":"Shipped in commits 12cbadb0 / 6c5c98ac.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential"]}
 {"id":"bd-10pq","title":"Cross-cutting: Architecture heterogeneity (aarch64/x86_64/RISC-V)","description":"Multi-ISA support: 31 ISA targets with different atomic/futex semantics. Priority: x86_64 (primary), aarch64 (active bring-up), RISC-V (future). Must handle: different atomic ordering guarantees, futex syscall variations, SIMD multiarch dispatch (IFUNC-equivalent). SOS barrier certificates must be ISA-independent.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:17.526480149Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T03:39:34.407831448Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T03:39:34.405902286Z","close_reason":"Architecture heterogeneity artifacts are present and verified: rch cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane --test sos_barrier_arch_independence_test passed 6/6.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","critique","cross-platform","feature-parity","frankenlibc","gap-closure","pthread","testing","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-10pq","depends_on_id":"bd-5vr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-10pq","depends_on_id":"bd-ldj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-11g","title":"RaptorQ Runtime: Pattern extraction from FrankenSQLite spec (systematic+repair+proof+tuning)","description":"Extract and restate the RaptorQ design pattern from FrankenSQLite in a runtime_math-compatible form.

Key spec concepts to port (pattern-level):
- Systematic fast path: read/store source symbols without GF(256) solve.
- Deterministic repair symbol generation from (ObjectId/epoch seed, K, symbol_size).
- Two overheads: additive decode slack + multiplicative loss budget.
- Optional adaptive overhead tuning using anytime-valid evidence (e-process) + evidence ledger.
- Explainable decode proofs on failure.

Deliverable:
- A short, self-contained design note (in bead comment) that future work can implement without re-reading the FrankenSQLite spec.","design":"Design note captured in-repo: crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/raptorq_pattern_frankensqlite.md (systematic+repair symbols, deterministic repair schedule, overhead formula, anytime-valid tuning hook, decode proofs; strict/hardened constraints).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltForge","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:34:56.102392443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T07:01:16.006607654Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T07:01:16.006484483Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":624,"issue_id":"bd-11g","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Design Note: RaptorQ Pattern (FrankenSQLite) Restated for glibc_rust Runtime

### Why We Want This In libc
We already have runtime_math controllers that make membrane decisions (Allow / FullValidate / Repair / Deny). When something bad happens we want an *audit-grade* trail that survives partial loss (ring-buffer overwrites, sampling, crashes, dropped events) and remains explainable.

RaptorQ's key “alien artifact” idea is **appendable redundancy**:
- emit cheap *systematic* symbols first (fast path)
- optionally emit deterministic *repair* symbols later (background cadence)
- decode and generate proofs offline (harness/tooling), not on the hot path

### Constraints (glibc_rust-specific)
- No `/dp/asupersync` runtime dependency. Runtime may implement a tiny encoder itself.
- Strict mode must not pay codec costs on the hot path.
- Hardened mode may pay bounded overhead but must remain deterministic and measurable.

### Portable Pattern (What We Copy)
1. **Source block / epoching**
   - Group a bounded window of evidence events into an *epoch*.
   - Epoch defines: `K_source` (number of systematic records), `T` (record/symbol size), and seed.
2. **Systematic fast path**
   - Emit/store raw evidence records as systematic symbols `ESI = 0..K_source-1`.
   - Layout should favor sequential capture/export (contiguous run) to minimize read overhead in tooling.
3. **Deterministic repair generation**
   - Generate repair symbols `ESI = K_source..K_source+R-1` deterministically from `(epoch_id, K_source, T, policy)`.
   - Determinism is the superpower: tooling can reproduce encoding schedule and validate.
4. **Overhead = additive slack + multiplicative budget**
   - Keep an additive decode slack `slack_decode` (FrankenSQLite V1 uses `+2`).
   - Add multiplicative overhead `overhead_percent` as needed.
5. **Anytime-valid tuning**
   - Use an anytime-valid monitor (e-process) to decide when to raise redundancy.
   - Record changes in an evidence ledger.
6. **Decode proofs**
   - When tooling decodes/repairs, it emits a proof artifact describing which symbols were used and why the decode is trusted.

### Concrete Formulas (From FrankenSQLite Spec Excerpts)
These are the “load-bearing” knobs we can reuse verbatim:

- Additive slack:
  - `slack_decode = 2` (default)

- Repair symbol count selection:
  - `R = max(slack_decode, ceil(K_source * overhead_percent / 100))`

- Back-of-envelope loss fraction tolerated (engineering approximation):
  - `loss_fraction_max ≈ max(0, (R - slack_decode) / (K_source + R))`

### Deterministic Seed Derivation (Portable Rule)
FrankenSQLite uses a deterministic seed derived from a stable identifier (e.g., changeset id):
- `seed = xxh3_64(id_bytes)`

glibc_rust mapping recommendation:
- Define an `epoch_id` that is stable within-process (and includes a monotonic counter).
- Derive seed from `(epoch_id || build_id || mode)` to avoid collisions across runs.

### Record/Envelope Mapping (What We Emit)
FrankenSQLite’s SymbolRecord idea maps cleanly to libc evidence:

- **EvidenceSymbolRecord** (proposal)
  - Header: `{ magic, version, epoch_id, seqno, family, mode, action, flags }`
  - Coding: `{ esi, k_source, r_repair, symbol_size_T, seed }`
  - Integrity: `{ payload_hash (xxh3_128 or blake3), prev_hash (hash-chain) }`
  - Payload: fixed-size `T` bytes (small; e.g., 64–256B), or padded.

Notes:
- “Auth tags” are optional: for libc evidence we may just do hash-chains + optional MAC in tooling.
- Source symbols should be cheap to write (memcpy into ring buffer + atomic seqno).
- Repair symbols should be generated on cadence (every N decisions, timer tick, or on exit).

### Where Decode Happens
- Runtime: encode only (systematic always; repair optional).
- Harness/tooling: decode, verify hashes/chain, emit DecodeProof.

### Open Design Choice (Explicit)
We must choose whether runtime implements:
- a small XOR-only fountain (LT-like) code (fastest, simplest), or
- a tighter RaptorQ-compatible schedule (harder), or
- “RaptorQ-inspired” parity that tooling decodes with a matching decoder.

This is decided in `bd-3a9`.

### Acceptance Criteria For The Pattern Extraction
- A concrete mapping of the above into glibc_rust terms (epoch, record format, seed, R selection).
- Explicit non-goals: no GF(256) Gaussian solve on hot path; no asupersync runtime dep.
- A minimal test matrix for future implementation:
  - drop/bitflip simulation
  - deterministic reproduction
  - decode-proof verification","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:50:28Z"}]}
@@ -815,6 +815,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-hmwxy","title":"PORT: fmtcheck (NetBSD/FreeBSD libutil printf-format compatibility check)","description":"NetBSD/FreeBSD libutil fmtcheck(user, default) returns user if its printf-format conversion specifiers are compatible with default; otherwise returns default. "Compatible" means same number of conversions AND same type-class for each (INT vs FP vs STR vs PTR vs N), with length modifiers normalized so that "%d" and "%i" interchange but "%d" vs "%ld" do NOT. Used by syslog wrappers and any tool that wants to safely accept a user-supplied format string as long as it does not change the variadic-argument shape. Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi. Ship: pure-safe core::stdlib::fmtcheck::compatible(user, default) -> bool that delegates to the existing parse_format_string + walks the FormatSpec list mapping (conversion, length) to a normalized ConvShape enum + thin abi shim that returns user-pointer or default-pointer per the BSD contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:00:26.442450279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:05:11.154259826Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:05:11.152360179Z","close_reason":"shipped: core::stdlib::fmtcheck (ConvShape + LenClass + compatible) + stdlib_abi::fmtcheck shim; 23+12 tests; clippy-clean; rch-built","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-hnec","title":"Add ELF loader fuzz target for parser and relocation surfaces","description":"Add a new cargo-fuzz target in crates/frankenlibc-fuzz for ELF loader parsing and related relocation/hash surfaces that are not currently fuzz-covered, with seed corpus entries and minimal documentation/comments as needed.","notes":"Added fuzz_elf_loader target, seed corpus, loader-domain harness metadata, and regenerated fuzz_harness_architecture.v1.json. Verified with targeted fuzz-crate check/clippy, harness test, and workspace check/clippy/fmt via rch. Agent Mail review delivery to MistyCliff attempted twice but failed due mailbox DB errors.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-16T22:06:53.739313078Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-16T22:20:52.012240065Z","closed_at":"2026-04-16T22:20:52.008566060Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ho3il","title":"[LOW] review: fprintf executor matrix lags snprintf coverage","description":"[LOW] review: fprintf executor matrix lags snprintf coverage

Recent printf pipeline changes expanded the snprintf/sprintf executor matrix, including `NullPtr` and several 3/4/5-argument combinations, but the fprintf path remains much narrower.

Current gap:
- `crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:2712-2849` handles `PrintfArg::NullPtr` and multiple 3+/4+/5-argument combinations for `run_impl_snprintf` / `run_host_snprintf`.
- `crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs:2854-2910` still only supports the small fprintf subset and returns `unsupported fprintf arg combination` for anything wider.

That creates a coverage skew inside the same executor family: newly-added fixture cases can pass through snprintf/sprintf while equivalent fprintf cases are silently untestable.

Review ask:
- keep the fprintf dispatch matrix in lockstep with the snprintf/sprintf executor surface
- add at least the currently-supported `NullPtr` and 3+/4+/5-argument combinations so harness coverage does not regress by API shape","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:29:44.457254443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T22:35:40.067098287Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T22:35:40.064806561Z","close_reason":"Verified parity: run_impl_snprintf/run_host_snprintf and run_impl_fprintf/run_host_fprintf matchers in crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs now carry identical arg-shape sets (17 match arms each; normalized diff shows EQUAL for both impl and host runners). NullPtr + 3/4/5-argument combinations present on both sides. Verified by:
  awk '/^fn run_impl_snprintf/,/^fn run_host_snprintf/' ... | grep -cE '^\s*\['  -> 17
  awk '/^fn run_impl_fprintf/,/^fn run_host_fprintf/' ... | grep -cE '^\s*\['    -> 17
  normalized sort/diff of arm shapes: EQUAL for both impl and host pairs.
End-to-end: rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_harness cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test printf_conformance_conformance_test -> 14/0 passed in 2.08s. Work landed in commits 1c5a9d0f + 9ea2ad56.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","coverage","printf","review"]}
+{"id":"bd-huug1","title":"PORT: __pthread_cond_wait/signal/broadcast + __pthread_kill + __pthread_key_delete (glibc reserved aliases)","description":"Port the remaining glibc reserved-namespace aliases for the pthread condvar / signal / TSD entry points: __pthread_cond_wait, __pthread_cond_signal, __pthread_cond_broadcast, __pthread_kill, __pthread_key_delete. glibc emits these underscored variants both as internal-use entry points and as the symbols libstdc++ links against for std::condition_variable, std::call_once thread cancellation cleanup, and TSD destructors. Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers that delegate to the public pthread_cond_wait/signal/broadcast, pthread_kill, pthread_key_delete in pthread_abi. Tests verify each alias produces the same observable behavior: condvar wake-up unblocks a waiting thread, kill delivers the signal to a target tid (we observe via pthread_sigmask + sigpending), and key_delete on a freshly-created key returns 0.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:34:10.301291885Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:34:10.301291885Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-hxfu0","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] execvp_via_execve walks unbounded user-supplied `file` (bd-z4k96 class)","description":"FOUND + FIXED via review round 5 (commit 1d68447a).

execvp_via_execve at L62 dereferenced the caller's `file` pointer via CStr::from_ptr with no length bound. A non-NUL-terminated pathname (attacker-controlled or memory-corrupted) walked arbitrary process memory.

Reachability: every program that resolves a command via PATH (shell builtins, system(), subprocess wrappers, container runtime exec helpers) hits this path with caller-supplied text.

Fix: read_bounded_cstr replacement; on None return -1 + ENOENT.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T07:45:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commits 0c0b59f1 / 1d68447a / fe3f1c06; 3120/3120 abi tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bd-z4k96-class","high","review","unbounded-cstr"]}
 {"id":"bd-hzkc6","title":"syscall: signalfd4 existing fd update with same mask should return same fd without touching errno","description":"Host glibc returns the same fd and leaves errno unchanged for signalfd(existing_fd, &mask, 0). Add focused ABI regression coverage for the in-place update contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T10:56:03.717758642Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:06:51.635816783Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:06:51.633658460Z","close_reason":"signalfd4(existing_fd, same_mask, 0) → same_fd (in-place update) pinned.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-i18zl","title":"PORT: strvis + strnvis + strunvis + strnunvis (NetBSD vis(3) family)","description":"NetBSD vis(3) family — visual byte encoding/decoding used by mtree, fpr, cd9660, pkgsrc, BSD pkg tools and many ports for safely round-tripping arbitrary binary data through ASCII-only channels (file names in tar/cpio listings, log lines). Ship the four most commonly used variants: strvis(dst, src, flags) encodes src into dst (NUL terminated) returning the number of bytes written; strnvis(dst, dlen, src, flags) is the bounded variant returning -1 if dst too small; strunvis(dst, src) and strnunvis(dst, dlen, src) decode the inverse. Default mode encodes non-printable bytes as \\^X (control) or \\M-X (high-bit) per BSD vis(3); with VIS_OCTAL flag (0x01) all non-printable bytes go to \\NNN. Other VIS_* flags ignored in v1. Currently missing entirely from frankenlibc-abi. Ship: pure-safe core::stdio::vis::{encode_byte, decode_one, strvis, strunvis} + abi shims that handle raw-pointer NUL and bounds.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:33:44.856900442Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T06:42:13.077049471Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T06:42:13.075032533Z","close_reason":"shipped: core::stdio::vis (encode_byte/decode_one + recursive M- handling) + stdio_abi strvis/strnvis/strunvis/strnunvis; 21+12 tests; clippy-clean; rch-built","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
